idx.auth.caption: Create an account below, then go to the Reviews page..
idx.auth.headline: Account for testers and reviewers
idx.brand.name: Measure Master
idx.cta.body: This app is already usable for real-world jobs.  We are iterating based on feedback from people who actually work on site.
idx.cta.play: Get it on Google Play Store
idx.cta.sub: If you have trouble installing, please check the How to use page first.
idx.cta.title: Try measuring with just a photo.
idx.features.f1.body: Place a reference rectangle with known dimensions (for example, A4 paper). Using that, the app corrects perspective and estimates length and area on the photo.
idx.features.f1.title: Fix distortion with a reference rectangle
idx.features.f2.body: Trace straight or curved boundaries with your finger.  The app adds up total length and area for each shape and for the overall selection.
idx.features.f2.title: Measure complex outlines
idx.features.f3.body: The interface minimizes steps and button count so you can operate it quickly even when you are on site, wearing gloves, or in a hurry.
idx.features.f3.title: Designed for small screens and quick use
idx.features.lead: From photo perspective correction to curved line length, Measure Master focuses on the tools you actually use in the field.
idx.features.title: Key features
idx.hero.main_title: Measure on site — no ruler needed.
idx.hero.play_banner: click here to download Measure Master in free
idx.hero.subtitle: Photo Perspective, AR Camera and On-screen.
idx.hero.tagline: Correct perspective in photos, measure real-world distances and draw shapes directly on your screen — all in one place.
idx.hero.title: All-in-one measuring app
idx.limits.lead: Measure Master aims to bring the feel of “tape measure + field sense” into your phone. Please keep the following in mind.
idx.limits.li1: It targets approximate values for decision-making, not certified engineering-grade measurements.
idx.limits.li2: The more accurate your reference rectangle dimensions are, the more reliable the results will be.
idx.limits.li3: Camera angle, photo quality, and lens distortion can all introduce error.
idx.limits.li4: For legal or contractual measurements, you must still use proper professional tools and procedures.
idx.limits.title: About accuracy and limitations
idx.meta.description: Measure real-world distances, correct perspective in photos, and draw shapes directly on your screen — all in one place.
idx.meta.title: Measure Master
idx.modes.ar.body: Measure real-time distance between two points indoors using the AR camera.
idx.modes.ar.title: AR Mode
idx.modes.pm.body: Estimate the actual perimeter or area in a photo using a basic reference rectangle and perspective correction.
idx.modes.pm.title: Photo Perspective Mode
idx.modes.sm.body: Draw shapes directly on your screen and get approximate length and area immediately.
idx.modes.sm.title: On-screen Mode
idx.screens.lead: These screenshots are the same ones used on the Google Play listing.
idx.screens.s1.alt: Set up a reference rectangle, then measure lengths and areas in the corrected view.
idx.screens.s1.cap: Set up a reference rectangle, then measure lengths and areas in the corrected view.
idx.screens.s2.alt: Use AR mode to measure a segment directly in 3D space.
idx.screens.s2.cap: Use AR mode to measure a segment directly in 3D space.
idx.screens.s3.alt: Configure units, reference rectangle values, and other options before you measure.
idx.screens.s3.cap: Configure units, reference rectangle values, and other options before you measure.
idx.screens.title: Screenshots
idx.who.c1.body: Take one photo on site instead of drawing a quick sketch. As long as you include a known rectangle (like A4 paper), you can revisit the photo later and estimate lengths and areas.
idx.who.c1.title: Constructors and remodelers
idx.who.c2.body: When you just need to know “about how many square meters this is,”  trace the outline and see the approximate area on the spot.
idx.who.c2.title: Doors, windows, flooring, and more
idx.who.c3.body: Overlay measurements directly on the photo, capture the screen, and share it as a simple report with clients or colleagues.
idx.who.c3.title: Photos with dimensions on top
idx.who.lead: It’s optimized not for ultra-precise engineering work, but for fast decisions on site
idx.who.title: For whom  is Measure Master ?
htu_001: Step 1. : Set Up Your Device (One-Time Setup)
htu_002: Before measuring, you need to calibrate your device once.
htu_003_c: to open the Sub Menu Bar.
htu_004: Tap the screen twice to draw a straight line and measure it with a ruler.
htu_005: The displayed length will not match the real size yet — this is normal.
htu_006_b: and enter the actual width of your phone screen (measure from the left edge to the right edge using a ruler).
htu_007: With this setting-up, the values will be equal between shown on the screen and actually measured. This setup only needs to be done once.
htu_010_a: Tap or drag to draw lines or shapes.
htu_011: When a shape is completed, the perimeter and area are calculated automatically.
htu_012: Spending even one minute per day counts as active usage during testing.
htu_013: Step 3. : Prepare a Reference Photo (Optional, for Higher Accuracy)
htu_014: To measure real-world objects more accurately:
htu_015: Place a square rectangle like A4 paper on a table or wall.
htu_016: Take a photo showing the rectangle and save the photo to your gallery.
htu_018_a: Load the saved photo from your gallery.
htu_020_b: page at first and return to
htu_021: Create 4 points by tapping near the four corners of the reference object (such as an A4 sheet).
htu_025_b: to meet with four corners exactly.
htu_022: Enter the actual width and height of the rectangle in the input fields.
htu_027: Screen values (mm) are converted into real-world measurements (cm, mm, etc.). This is the core purpose of the app.
htu_123_hom: Home Page Links>AR Mode>RightEye>Submenu>LeftEye>Load Photo>Settings>Side Toolbar
htu_125_str: Straight / Curved Line>Paint / Erase>Move Points>Reset> Undo>Redo>Save
htu_133_sho: Show / Hide>Grid>Rotate Right>Rotate Left>Move Canvas>Zoom In>Zoom Out>1:1 View
htu_162_new: New starter should set up Zeroing first of all.
htu_163_tab: Users of tablet or desk-top need new setting Canvas Ratio especially
htu_212_aft: After measuring the red reference rectangle in AR, calculate the blue painted area.
htu_213_you: You can also calculate white and light gray painted areas together.
htu_222_use: Use this when AR is unavailable, or when estimating dimensions later at the office.
htu_223_lay: Please lay the reference rectangle at the center of the photo. Then, the measure variation can be minimized.
htu_241_rea: Real-World Example: Measuring an Apartment Building
htu_242_bal: The size of a single balcony (325 × 275 cm) is used as a real-world reference.
htu_243_ano: A rectangle is drawn for the balcony and another for the full building façade.
htu_244_bas: Based on perspective estimation, the building’s total size is calculated as 3,124 cm (width) and 6,119 cm (height).
htu_245_nex: Next, this building dimensions are used to calculate the exterior wall area for painting, with totals organized by color.
htu_332_tab: Measure a table’s width on site (within AR range).
htu_img_340_alt: Measure a TV Monitor with AR
htu_342_tvs: Measure a TV’s screen width (within AR range).
htu_412_act: Actual width is of 85 cm and estimated 161 cm > 103 cm > 85 cm with big variation.
htu_413_nea: The nearest line shows 85 cm, but the far line shows 161 cm because the basic rectangle was drawn incorrectly.
htu_414_how: How can you reduce this difference?
htu_422_act: Actual width is of 85 cm and estimated 87 cm > 85 cm, 85 cm with little variation.
htu_423_dra: Draw another rectangle as large as possible on the same plane.<very important factor>
htu_424_thn: Then the larger rectangle becomes the new base, and each length becomes almost equal.
htu_500_exa: Example of measuring by double rectangles
htu_511_hor: Horizontal Length of a window glass
htu_512_1975: Actual width of the window glass = 1975mm
htu_513_1987: Estimated 1987mm   + 12mm  0.6% plus  by measuring with double rectangle.
htu_514_2033: Estimated 2033mm   + 58mm  2.9% plus  by measuring with single rectangle.
htu_521_ver: Vertical Length of a window glass
htu_522_2150: Actual height of the window glass = 2150mm.
htu_523_2141: Estimated 2141mm   -  9mm  0.4% minus  by measuring with double rectangle.
htu_524_2137: Estimated 2137mm   - 13mm  0.6% minus  by measuring with single rectangle.
htu_612_non: There is no need to manually place a reference square before taking a photo.
htu_613_usi: Using Measure Master’s built-in AR camera, a perspective square is automatically generated and embedded into the image at the moment of capture.
htu_622_whn: When the image is loaded in the RightEye page, the reference square can be instantly applied by tapping the trapezoid icon,
htu_623_dim: and its real-world dimensions (W, H) are automatically filled in.
htu_624_siz: The size of the reference square can also be selected by the user in Settings.
htu_632_wor: 1. Works only with images captured using Measure Master’s built-in AR camera (images from other AR camera apps are not supported).
htu_633_ava: 2. Available only on the RightEye page.
htu_634_req: 3. Requires an ARCore-supported device. at least Android 16 or up.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Measure Master ("we", "us", or "our") values your privacy and complies with applicable privacy laws and regulations.
privacy.section1.body: When you sign up or log in, we collect your email address and password.
privacy.section2.body: To identify users and manage accounts, and to provide the service (login and posting reviews).
privacy.section3.body: We retain personal information only for as long as necessary to provide the service, and delete it without delay once the purpose is fulfilled, unless retention is required by law.
privacy.section4.body: We use cloud services such as Supabase (authentication/database) and Vercel (hosting).
privacy.section5.body: You may request access to, correction of, or deletion of your personal information, and withdraw consent (account deletion).
privacy.section6.body: To keep you signed in, we may use local/session storage or cookies.
privacy.section7.body: We use HTTPS for data in transit, restrict access, and follow the principle of minimal collection.
privacy.section9.body: If this policy changes, we will post the update on this page with the updated date.
privacy.ar.title: Privacy handling for AR Measuring (Camera) features
privacy.ar.section1.li1: We do not collect, store, or transmit personal data to our servers.
privacy.ar.section1.li2: Camera (CAMERA): Camera frames are processed on-device for real-time AR measuring.
privacy.ar.section1.li3: Photos/Media access (optional): Used only when you choose to load an image from your gallery.
privacy.ar.section1.li4: Photo saving (optional): Saved to your device gallery only when you choose to save a capture.
privacy.ar.section2.li1: Camera frames are processed on-device only and are not transmitted to any server.
privacy.ar.section2.li2: Only captures you choose to save are stored in your device gallery, and you can delete them anytime from your gallery.
privacy.ar.section3.li1: We do not share personal data with third parties.
privacy.ar.section3.li2: We may use Google ARCore, which is an on-device AR tracking library and does not collect or transmit personal data for this feature.
privacy.ar.section4.li1: The app does not transmit personal data over the network.
privacy.ar.section4.li2: Saved images are protected by the operating system security mechanisms (Scoped Storage/MediaStore).
privacy.ar.section5.li1: We do not collect personal data from children.
privacy.ar.section6.li1: You can delete saved captures at any time from your gallery.
privacy.ar.section7.li1: If features change or laws are updated, we will update this section and revise the "Last updated" date at the top of this page.
